openmeter/ledger/account.purpose: Domain-object core of the ledger: defines Account, SubAccount, Address, and Balance types that implement the ledger.* interfaces, plus the Repo (persistence) and Service (orchestration) contracts. All higher ledger packages (resolvers, historical, transactions, breakage) build on the types defined here.

patterns.name: Data/Live split with construction-time injection
patterns.description: *Data structs are plain DTOs; Account/SubAccount embed the data and receive AccountLiveServices (SubAccountService) only via NewAccountFromData / NewSubAccountFromData. Never set live services by field assignment after construction.
patterns.example: NewAccountFromData(data, AccountLiveServices{SubAccountService: svc})

patterns.name: Type-dispatching constructor
patterns.description: NewAccountFromData switches on AccountType and returns typed wrappers (CustomerFBOAccount, CustomerReceivableAccount, CustomerAccruedAccount, BusinessAccount). Callers type-assert on the returned ledger.Account.
patterns.example: fboAcc, ok := acc.(ledger.CustomerFBOAccount)

patterns.name: EnsureSubAccount upsert via canonical route
patterns.description: Sub-accounts are created idempotently through services.SubAccountService.EnsureSubAccount keyed on the canonical routing key; never call a raw create.
patterns.example: svc.EnsureSubAccount(ctx, ledger.CreateSubAccountInput{Namespace: ns, AccountID: id, Route: params.Route()})

patterns.name: Validate before repo/service calls
patterns.description: Every input and RouteParams type exposes Validate(); call it (GetSubAccountForRoute calls params.Validate() first) before any persistence call.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertions
patterns.description: Each concrete type carries a var _ ledger.X = (*Type)(nil) line — the only compile-time proof of interface compliance. Do not remove.
patterns.example: var _ ledger.CustomerFBOAccount = (*CustomerFBOAccount)(nil)

patterns.name: SubAccountService self-wiring in service.New()
patterns.description: account/service.New() wires SubAccountService as a forward reference to itself; do not inject it externally — the self-wiring is load-bearing.
patterns.example: svc := &service{}; svc.live.SubAccountService = svc; return svc

key_file_guides.role: Account domain object + AccountData DTO + NewAccountFromData type-dispatcher and AccountLiveServices container.
key_file_guides.watch_for: nil SubAccountService panics on GetSubAccountForRoute; populate AccountLiveServices fully at construction.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerFBO/Receivable/Accrued wrappers; each routes via EnsureSubAccount after params.Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: RouteParams missing Currency error on Validate(); accrued is routed by currency only.

key_file_guides.role: BusinessAccount wrapper for wash/earnings/brokerage/breakage; newBusinessAccount only valid for those types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: newBusinessAccount is only safe inside NewAccountFromData's business-type case.

key_file_guides.role: SubAccount domain object; NewSubAccountFromData builds the Address from RouteMeta.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Empty SubAccountID or RouteID errors during address construction.

key_file_guides.role: Address wraps AddressData and prebuilds SubAccountRoute; implements ledger.PostingAddress with field-by-field Equal().
key_file_guides.watch_for: RouteID and SubAccountID both required; Equal() is value comparison, not pointer.

key_file_guides.role: Repo interface embedding entutils.TxCreator with CreateAccount/EnsureSubAccount/Get/List methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Implementations must satisfy the full TxCreator triad for TransactingRepo to work.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface aliasing ledger.AccountCatalog + ledger.AccountLocker; input types re-exported from ledger.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Service returns ledger.Account/ledger.SubAccount domain objects, not *Data DTOs.

anti_patterns: Returning *AccountData / *SubAccountData from Service methods — callers expect live domain objects with services attached.
Setting live.SubAccountService externally instead of relying on service.New() self-wiring.
Manually building AddressData.RoutingKey strings instead of ledger.BuildRoutingKey — breaks canonical uniqueness.
Removing var _ ledger.X = (*T)(nil) compile-time assertions.
Using context.Background()/context.TODO() in any method — propagate the caller ctx.

decisions.decision: AccountLiveServices is a value type injected at construction; SubAccountService is a forward reference so the service self-registers.
decisions.rationale: Avoids a global service registry and resolves the chicken-and-egg dependency of the service needing itself as the SubAccountService.

decisions.decision: Sub-accounts are keyed by a canonical routing key derived from the Route value.
decisions.rationale: Guarantees idempotent upsert: two callers asking for the same route converge on one row without races.

code_examples.scenario: Provision a sub-account for a customer FBO account

openmeter/ledger/breakage.purpose: Credit-expiration breakage sub-domain: keeps future credit-expiration ledger entries aligned with actual customer credit usage via plan/release/reopen breakage records. It is an allocation/index layer over the ledger (the ledger remains the accounting source of truth); the breakage/adapter child persists LedgerBreakageRecord rows.

patterns.name: Service produces TransactionInputs + PendingRecords
patterns.description: PlanIssuance/ReleasePlan/ReopenRelease return ledger.TransactionInput(s) plus PendingRecord metadata; the caller commits the ledger group, then PersistCommittedRecords durably writes the records.
patterns.example: txs, pending, err := svc.PlanIssuance(ctx, input); ...; svc.PersistCommittedRecords(ctx, pending, group)

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before NewService
patterns.description: NewService validates that Adapter, Dependencies.AccountService and Dependencies.AccountCatalog are non-nil before constructing the service.
patterns.example: if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Validate() on every input
patterns.description: All *Input types Validate() using ledger.ValidateTransactionAmount/ValidateCurrency/ValidateCostBasis/ValidateCreditPriority; service methods validate before doing work.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return ... }

patterns.name: Shared FBO-consumption == breakage-release ordering
patterns.description: Plans, releases and reopens follow credit_priority asc, expires_at asc, stable cursor asc — the same order the FBO collector consumes credit. Diverging silently corrupts which expiry is released.
patterns.example: ListPlans returns plans in the order the FBO collector must consume expiring credit

patterns.name: Breakage-impact netting
patterns.description: ListExpiredBreakageImpacts groups expired records by (expiresAt, currency) and computes impact = -(plans - releases + reopens); zero-impact groups are hidden and a negative internal total is an error.
patterns.example: group.amount.Neg() with cursorID set to the smallest plan record ID

patterns.name: NoopService for disabled breakage
patterns.description: NoopService implements Service with all-nil/empty returns for deployments/tests wiring credit purchases without expiration support.
patterns.example: var _ Service = NoopService{}

key_file_guides.role: Service interface, Config + Validate, service struct holding adapter + transactions.ResolverDependencies, and the PlanIssuance/ReleasePlan/ReopenRelease inputs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Dependencies.AccountService and AccountCatalog are mandatory; ImmediateReleaseAmount must be 0..Amount.

key_file_guides.role: ListExpiredBreakageImpacts: nets plan/release/reopen records into customer-visible BreakageImpact rows with stable cursor windowing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A negative netted amount or a group with no plan record is an error, not a silent skip; sorts by descending cursor.

key_file_guides.role: Record, Plan, Release, PendingRecord, BreakageImpact and list input/result types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All breakage record amounts are positive — the sign lives in the ledger entries.

key_file_guides.role: NoopService no-op implementation and NewNoopService constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with the Service interface so credits-disabled wiring compiles.

key_file_guides.role: Authoritative spec of plan/release/reopen/advance-backfill semantics and the core ordering invariant.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Read before changing any breakage math — the correctness argument depends on the shared ordering.

anti_patterns: Building ledger TransactionInputs by hand instead of going through transactions.* / the breakage service.
Diverging breakage release order from the FBO collection order (credit_priority, expires_at, stable cursor).
Treating breakage-generated FBO entries as normal credit issuance/usage instead of marking ledger.breakage.kind.
Persisting records before the corresponding ledger group has committed (must use PersistCommittedRecords post-commit).
Putting Ent/persistence access in this package instead of breakage/adapter — this layer is service logic only.

decisions.decision: Breakage records are a positive-amount allocation/index layer separate from the ledger entries.
decisions.rationale: The ledger stays the source of truth; records only let later flows find open plans, reopen releases, and project customer-visible expired credit.

decisions.decision: Correctness relies on FBO consumption order matching breakage release order rather than grant-level lineage.
decisions.rationale: Avoids per-grant lineage; the shared ordering makes the open planned amount at an expiry exactly the remaining unused expiring credit.

code_examples.scenario: Plan breakage for newly issued expiring credit and persist after commit

openmeter/ledger/historical.purpose: The double-entry ledger engine: the Ledger struct implements ledger.Ledger (CommitGroup, GetTransactionGroup, ListTransactions) and ledger.BalanceQuerier (account/sub-account balances) over the Repo (historical/adapter) which books LedgerTransaction + LedgerEntry rows. Owns transaction/entry/balance domain reconstruction.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps the full CommitGroup sequence
patterns.description: CommitGroup validates routing, then inside one transaction.Run block locks accounts, validates balances, creates the group, and books each transaction so all phases roll back atomically. Never split phases or call BookTransaction outside the block.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, l.repo, func(ctx context.Context) (*TransactionGroup, error) { ... })

patterns.name: Pre-lock all accounts before writes
patterns.description: lockAccountsForTransactionInputs collects every sub-account's parent account and calls accountLocker.LockAccountsForPosting (sorted NamespacedID ordering) before any balance check or insert.
patterns.example: if err := l.lockAccountsForTransactionInputs(ctx, group.Namespace(), txInputs); err != nil { ... }

patterns.name: Validate inputs before repo delegation
patterns.description: ListTransactions, SumEntries and CommitGroup call Validate()/params.Validate() and return early before touching the repo.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return ..., fmt.Errorf("...: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Domain reconstruction via constructors
patterns.description: Transaction and Entry are always built via NewTransactionFromData / newEntryFromData (which calls account.NewAddressFromData) — never instantiate the structs directly.
patterns.example: tx, err := NewTransactionFromData(txData, entryDataSlice)

patterns.name: Tri-tuple cursor via Transaction.Cursor()
patterns.description: TransactionCursor carries (BookedAt, CreatedAt, ID); always produce cursors through Transaction.Cursor(), never build TransactionCursor manually.
patterns.example: func (t *Transaction) Cursor() ledger.TransactionCursor { return ledger.TransactionCursor{...} }

patterns.name: Routing validation before booking
patterns.description: CommitGroup runs ledger.ValidateTransactionInputWith(ctx, txInput, l.routingValidator) per transaction before the transaction.Run block.
patterns.example: if err := ledger.ValidateTransactionInputWith(ctx, txInput, l.routingValidator); err != nil { ... }

key_file_guides.role: Ledger struct + NewLedger constructor; implements CommitGroup, ListTransactions, GetTransactionGroup and the account-locking flow.
key_file_guides.watch_for: validateAccountBalancesForTransaction is a TODO no-op today; do not assume balance enforcement at commit.

key_file_guides.role: Repo interface (TxCreator + CreateTransactionGroup/GetTransactionGroup/BookTransaction/SumEntries/ListTransactions) and DTOs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Repo implementations must eagerly load SubAccount→Account→Route edges or hydration fails.

key_file_guides.role: Transaction / TransactionGroup domain objects; NewTransactionFromData builds entries.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Cursor() is the only correct way to produce a TransactionCursor.

key_file_guides.role: Entry domain object; newEntryFromData builds the PostingAddress via account.NewAddressFromData.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EntryData needs SubAccountID, AccountType, RouteID, RouteKey, RouteKeyVer or address construction errors.

key_file_guides.role: Balance struct + GetAccountBalance/GetSubAccountBalance delegating to sumEntries.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SumEntries returns the same value for Settled and Pending — no settled/pending separation yet.

anti_patterns: Calling repo.BookTransaction outside the transaction.Run block — loses lock and validation atomicity.
Skipping ledger.ValidateTransactionInputWith before booking — invalid account-type combinations persist silently.
Mapping db rows to domain structs inline instead of via newEntryFromData / NewTransactionFromData.
Constructing TransactionCursor manually instead of via Transaction.Cursor().
Using context.Background() instead of the caller ctx — drops the Ent transaction and OTel spans.

decisions.decision: SumEntries returns identical SettledSum and PendingSum.
decisions.rationale: The historical ledger has no pending/settled split yet; a TODO in ledger.go flags the future divergence.

decisions.decision: Tri-tuple cursor (bookedAt, createdAt, ID) instead of a single auto-increment offset.
decisions.rationale: Stable across concurrent inserts sharing a booked_at; ID is a tie-breaker requiring no sequence column.

openmeter/ledger/resolvers.purpose: Bridges the ledger account layer to customer and namespace lifecycle: AccountResolver provisions customer FBO/Receivable/Accrued and business Wash/Earnings/Brokerage accounts on demand, CustomerLedgerHook wires account creation into customer.PostCreate, and namespaceHandler provisions business accounts on namespace creation. Only wired when credits.enabled=true.

patterns.name: transaction.Run + provisioning lock
patterns.description: CreateCustomerAccounts and EnsureBusinessAccounts open transaction.Run, acquire a provisioning advisory lock (lockr.LockForTX with a 5s bounded wait), then create missing accounts. Always run inside transaction.Run even when Locker is nil so ctx propagates.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.Repo, func(ctx context.Context) (ledger.CustomerAccounts, error) { s.lockCustomerProvisioning(ctx, customerID); ... })

patterns.name: Idempotent create via typed conflict error
patterns.description: CreateCustomerAccounts catches CustomerAccountAlreadyExistsError (via AsCustomerAccountAlreadyExistsError) from concurrent inserts and reuses the existing AccountID; the adapter must return this typed error on unique-constraint violations.
patterns.example: if existingErr, ok := AsCustomerAccountAlreadyExistsError(err); ok { accountIDs[accountType] = existingErr.AccountID; continue }

patterns.name: Hook embeds NoopCustomerLedgerHook
patterns.description: customerLedgerHook overrides only PostCreate; all other ServiceHook methods come from the embedded NoopCustomerLedgerHook.
patterns.example: type customerLedgerHook struct { NoopCustomerLedgerHook; config CustomerLedgerHookConfig }

patterns.name: namespaceHandler accepts a businessAccountProvisioner interface
patterns.description: NewNamespaceHandler takes an interface, not *AccountResolver, so wiring does not depend on the concrete type.
patterns.example: func NewNamespaceHandler(provisioner businessAccountProvisioner) namespace.Handler

patterns.name: Typed-account assertion on read
patterns.description: GetCustomerAccounts type-asserts each fetched account to its ledger.CustomerXAccount interface and errors on mismatch; missing mappings return ledger.ErrCustomerAccountMissing.
patterns.example: fboAccount, ok := fboAcc.(ledger.CustomerFBOAccount)

key_file_guides.role: AccountResolver — implements ledger.AccountResolver; provisioning locks and idempotent customer/business account creation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Locker is optional (nil-check before LockForTX) but always wrap in transaction.Run for ctx propagation.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerAccountRepo interface (TxCreator + CreateCustomerAccount + GetCustomerAccountIDs) and CreateCustomerAccountInput.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adapter must return CustomerAccountAlreadyExistsError on unique-constraint violations, not a raw DB error.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerAccountAlreadyExistsError, ErrCustomerAccountConflict (HTTP 409 ValidationIssue), AsCustomerAccountAlreadyExistsError unwrap helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use the unwrap helper in CreateCustomerAccounts to handle concurrent inserts.

key_file_guides.role: customerLedgerHook with PostCreate calling CreateCustomerAccounts; NewCustomerLedgerHook constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Register via customerService.RegisterHooks only when credits.enabled=true in app/common.

key_file_guides.role: namespaceHandler: CreateNamespace calls EnsureBusinessAccounts, DeleteNamespace is a no-op.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must be registered before initNamespace() in cmd/server/main.go or the default namespace lacks business accounts.

anti_patterns: Registering CustomerLedgerHook or namespaceHandler when credits.enabled=false — use ledgernoop.* variants instead.
Calling GetCustomerAccounts before CreateCustomerAccounts for a new customer — returns ErrCustomerAccountMissing.
Constructing AccountResolver without a Locker in production — concurrent provisioning creates duplicate accounts.
Adding balance/route business logic to resolvers/adapter — it belongs in the AccountResolver service layer.
Using context.Background() in hooks or the namespace handler — severs OTel traces and transaction context.

decisions.decision: Provisioning lock uses a 5s bounded wait converted to lockr.ErrLockTimeout on deadline.
decisions.rationale: Multi-service startup races fail fast instead of blocking indefinitely.

decisions.decision: CustomerAccountRepo is a minimal two-method interface (+ TxCreator), not a full Ent client.
decisions.rationale: Lets AccountResolver be unit-tested with a mock repo without a Postgres instance.

code_examples.scenario: Wire CustomerLedgerHook and namespace handler when credits are enabled
code_examples.code:
hook, err := resolvers.NewCustomerLedgerHook(resolvers.CustomerLedgerHookConfig{Service: accountResolver, Tracer: tracer})
if err != nil { return err }
customerService.RegisterHooks(hook)
ns.RegisterHandler(resolvers.NewNamespaceHandler(accountResolver))

openmeter/ledger/transactions.purpose: The transaction-template layer of the double-entry ledger: named value-struct templates (CustomerTransactionTemplate, OrgTransactionTemplate) resolve abstract intents into concrete ledger.TransactionInput values, with a correction path for reversing posted templates. This is the ONLY layer that constructs EntryInput/TransactionInput — callers always pass templates to ResolveTransactions.

patterns.name: Private typeGuard() seals the template interface
patterns.description: Every template implements typeGuard() guard returning an unexported type so templates cannot be defined outside this package.
patterns.example: func (t IssueCustomerReceivableTemplate) typeGuard() guard { return true }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion per template
patterns.description: Each template carries a var _ CustomerTransactionTemplate = (T{}) line; missing it means the template silently fails to dispatch.
patterns.example: var _ CustomerTransactionTemplate = (TransferCustomerFBOToAccruedTemplate{})

patterns.name: Validate() before resolve()
patterns.description: ResolveTransactions calls template.Validate() (using ledger.ValidateTransactionAmount/ValidateCurrency/ValidateCostBasis/ValidateCreditPriority) for every template before resolve().
patterns.example: if err := ledger.ValidateTransactionAmount(t.Amount); err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("amount: %w", err) }

patterns.name: nil from resolve() means skip (no-op)
patterns.description: When a template has nothing to post, resolve() returns (nil, nil); ResolveTransactions skips nil inputs rather than appending or erroring.
patterns.example: if len(t.Sources) == 0 { return nil, nil }

patterns.name: Auto-annotate produced inputs with direction
patterns.description: ResolveTransactions wraps each non-nil input via annotateTemplateTransaction(..., TransactionDirectionForward); CorrectTransaction uses TransactionDirectionCorrection.
patterns.example: annotateTemplateTransaction(tx, template, ledger.TransactionDirectionForward)

patterns.name: Register templates in transactionTemplateByName
patterns.description: CorrectTransaction reconstructs templates from the annotation-stored template name; every new template adds a case in correction.go (or returns templateCorrectionNotImplemented).
patterns.example: case templateName(IssueCustomerReceivableTemplate{}): return IssueCustomerReceivableTemplate{}, nil

patterns.name: Deterministic FBO collection helper
patterns.description: Templates draining FBO balances use collectFromPrioritizedCustomerFBO (sorts by CreditPriority asc, then SubAccountID) — never inline custom FBO iteration.
patterns.example: collections, err := collectFromPrioritizedCustomerFBO(ctx, customerID, t.Currency, t.Amount, resolvers)

key_file_guides.role: TransactionTemplate / CustomerTransactionTemplate / OrgTransactionTemplate interfaces and the private guard type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: typeGuard() returns a private type — templates cannot be implemented outside this package.

key_file_guides.role: ResolveTransactions entry point: validates scope, calls Validate(), dispatches resolve(), auto-annotates outputs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ResolverDependencies needs both AccountService (ledger.AccountResolver) and SubAccountService (ledgeraccount.Service).

key_file_guides.role: CorrectTransaction reconstructs the original template from annotations; transactionTemplateByName is the dispatch table.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Correcting a correction is rejected; a missing dispatch case only surfaces at correction time.

key_file_guides.role: EntryInput/TransactionInput/TransactionGroupInput concrete structs + GroupInputs() wrapper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EntryInput fields are unexported; use lo.Map to coerce []*EntryInput to []ledger.EntryInput.

key_file_guides.role: collectFromPrioritizedCustomerFBO / collectFromAttributableCustomerAccrued balance-collection helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: collectFromAttributableCustomerAccrued skips nil-CostBasis sub-accounts intentionally.

key_file_guides.role: Accrual templates (e.g. TransferCustomerFBOToAccruedTemplate) preserving cost-basis routing into accrued.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolveAccruedSubAccByRoutePairingKey aggregates by costBasisKey() (decimal string or 'null').

key_file_guides.role: Customer-scoped templates for the FBO/receivable/coverage lifecycle.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Some templates return templateCorrectionNotImplemented — not every template is correctable.

anti_patterns: Constructing EntryInput/TransactionInput outside this package — all posting construction belongs here.
Adding a template without a transactionTemplateByName case — it becomes unresolvable at correction time.
Using context.Background() in resolve()/correct() — propagate the ctx from ResolveTransactions/CorrectTransaction.
Bypassing collectFromPrioritizedCustomerFBO with custom FBO iteration — priority ordering must stay deterministic.
Implementing TransactionTemplate outside this package — the private guard seals the interface.

decisions.decision: Templates are value structs with unexported resolve()/correct() methods, not functions.
decisions.rationale: Colocates template logic, enables interface-type dispatch, and forces callers through the Validate()+annotation pipeline.

decisions.decision: Correction dispatches via the annotation-stored template name string, not a type registry.
decisions.rationale: Persisted transactions carry annotations; the correction path must rebuild the template from a stored string.

decisions.decision: nil from resolve() means skip, not error.
decisions.rationale: Conditional templates (e.g. no FBO balance) let ResolveTransactions accumulate only real postings without pre-checks.

code_examples.scenario: Resolve and commit a customer receivable issuance
